 to support our Technical Service operations across all Nordic countries. In this role, you will ensure the seamless coordination of service activities, maintain accurate device records, manage spare parts and inventories, and guarantee excellent customer support.
   

  

 If you enjoy structure, precision, and being at the center of service operations, this role is for you.
   

  

 ⭐
 **What You Will Do**
 In close partnership with field engineers and customer-facing teams, you will be responsible for:
   

  

**Customer Service & Coordination**
 Provide professional support to customers and internal teams via phone and email.
   

  

 Handle incoming service requests and assign them efficiently to field engineers.
   

  

 Ensure timely and accurate logging of all customer interactions.
   

  

**Operations & Administration**
 Maintain up-to-date electronic records for all devices.
   

  

 Prepare reports and assist in resolving service reporting issues.
   

  

 Coordinate device deliveries and manage stock levels.
   

  

 Support field engineers with purchase orders and invoice processing.
   

  

 Assist in annual inventory activities.
   

  

**Finance & Compliance**
 Prepare and execute customer invoicing.
   

  

 Keep documentation and registers accurate at all times.
   

  

 Adhere to all environmental, health, and safety SOPs, as well as internal Technical Service procedures.
   

  

**General Responsibilities**
 Maintain clear records and produce required reports.
   

  

 Perform additional duties as needed with minimal supervision.
   

  

 🎯
 **What Makes You Successful in This Role**
 Strong customer service mindset
   

  

 Fluent in Swedish and English (Finnish or Norwegian a bonus)
   

  

 Proficiency in MS Office (Word, Excel, Outlook)
   

  

 Excellent attention to detail and high accuracy
   

  

 Ability to manage multiple tasks and priorities
   

  

 Comfortable working independently
   

  

 Strong communication and coordination skills
   

  

 Strong problem solving skills and solutions orientated.
   

  

 Basic understanding of finance administration
   

  

 Hybrid working arrangement - 3 days in the office / 2 days home
   

  

**Reasonable Accommodation**
 Vantive is committed to working with and providing reasonable accommodations to individuals with disabilities globally. If, because of a medical condition or disability, you need a reasonable accommodation for any part of the application or interview process, please click on the link here and let us know the nature of your request along with your contact information. Form Link
   

  

**Recruitment Fraud Notice**
 Vantive has discovered incidents of employment scams, where fraudulent parties pose as Vantive employees, recruiters, or other agents, and engage with online job seekers in an attempt to steal personal and/or financial information. To learn how you can protect yourself, review our Recruitment Fraud Notice.

title: Programme Internship Middle East and North Africa at Kvinna till Kvinna, Stockholm
description:
**About The Internship**
 Do you want to contribute to Kvinna till Kvinna’s work focusing on programming and support to women’s rights organisations and women human rights defenders in the MENA region? Are you interested in learning more about programme/grants management, civil society support, and women’s rights in the MENA region?
   

  

 As an intern in the Grants Management Unit in Stockholm, you will contribute to our work by helping us in grants management, and programme administration, including tasks related to reporting, compliance and administrative support to programme activities. You may also be involved in contributing to various analyses relating to the situation for women human rights defenders and women’s rights organisations in the MENA region.
   

  

 The internship is intended to make a practical contribution to the work of the organisation through fulfilling identified duties and responsibilities, and to offer a learning opportunity for you as an intern. Through this internship, you will gain experience of planning, implementation, reporting and development of programmes and grants. You will be supported by, and report to, the Grants Manager for MENA. The specific tasks will be agreed between the Intern and the Grants Manager and depending on the profile and interests of the intern, as well as the needs of the MENA team.
   

  

 Additionally, you will be part of a group of other interns, each doing their internship in different areas of the organisation.
   

  

**Deadline for applications: April 6th, 2026**
*The Kvinna till Kvinna Foundation has defended women’s rights since 1993. For every woman’s right to be safe and to be heard. Today we are one of the world’s leading women’s rights organisations, working directly in areas affected by war and conflict to strengthen women’s influence and power. We work closely together with over 150 local partner organisations across 20 countries to end violence against women, reach lasting peace and close the gender gap once and for all. The future is equal. And together, we are change.*
**Key tasks will include:**
* Participate in the development of funding applications and results reports to institutional donors, including support to programme administration and compliance.
* Provide support in other daily programme management activities as needed.
* Participate in relevant departmental meetings and internal trainings/workshops.
* Conduct other tasks relevant to the Grants Management Unit and other departments, including general administrative tasks.
* Other tasks may be identified during the internship.


**About You**
 You are committed to gender equality and women’s rights. You are analytical, responsive, cooperative and self-driven. You have good organisational ability and can adapt to changing circumstances.
   

  

**Requirements:**
* Knowledge of women’s rights;
* Knowledge of and interest in civil society and women’s rights movements;
* Knowledge of and interest in the MENA region;
* Good IT skills (Microsoft Office package and internet platforms);
* Very good language and writing skills; English is mandatory, Arabic is an added advantage.
* Any previous experience of administration and reporting is considered as an added advantage.


 We prefer that you are looking for an internship as part of your current studies where you receive reimbursement from CSN or other government funds. If this does not apply to you, you need to mention how your internship is funded.
   

  

**Terms & Conditions And What We Offer**
 At Kvinna till Kvinna you will get a chance to be a part of an inspiring workplace with dedicated employees. During your time with us you will get the opportunity to work independently as well as in a team. You will deepen your knowledge of international development work, women's role for democracy and peace, planning/follow-up and reporting work. You will also be able to participate in both internal and external seminars/lectures that may be of interest.
   

  

 The internship is unpaid and covers full-time (37,5 hrs/week) for approx. 20 weeks starting 1 September 2026. Start and end date are negotiable.
   

  

 We will sign an internship agreement, and you will have insurance for the duration of your internship. The internship is based at the office in Stockholm.
   

  

 Diversity is very important to Kvinna till Kvinna, and we strive to reflect this in our recruitment.
   

  

**Application**
 Deadline for the application is April 6th, 2026.
   

  

 Submit your application, including cover letter and resume via the application button. Please mention how your internship is funded.
   

  

 The application should be written in English.
   

  

**Questions**
 If you have any questions regarding the position, please contact Johanna Remes, Grants Manager MENA johanna.remes@kvinnatillkvinna.se

title: Conversion Analyst to Procter & Gamble
description:
*We are currently looking for a Conversion Analyst to our exciting customer Procter & Gamble. This is a consultancy opportunity, a 6-month contract starting in the beginning of May 2026, with the possibility for an extension. The office is located in Solna, Stockholm.* 




  





**The Company** 




 P&G is a fortune 500 company in the FMCG industry. It is the home of iconic, trusted brands that make every-day life a little bit easier. The insight, innovation and passion of their talented teams has helped them to grow to a global company that supports good causes and protects the environment.
 



  





**The Role:** 




 We are looking for a detail-oriented Conversion Analyst to help support our Product Master Data Team.
 



  





 In this execution-focused role, you will be supporting new product launches and managing data changes for existing products. The scope focuses on data entry, issue identification and resolution, and master data accuracy management.
 



  





 You will primarily work with SAP and Excel to update, set up, and communicate essential master data for new product launches. Additionally, you will utilise Power BI reports to effectively prioritise your tasks.
 



  





 Your typical working day will be divided into two:
 



  





**Mornings** 
 : assisting the team in troubleshooting and resolving issues within our daily order cycle. This requires swift root cause analysis and prompt action to meet customer expectations.
 



**Afternoons** 
 : engaging in a variety of tasks to ensure that product master data is accurate and communicated effectively, thereby supporting the launch of new products. This involves working with the Global Data Synchronisation Network (GDSN), orientation to detail, and accuracy.
 



  





 This role is ideal for someone with strong communication and analytical skills. A successful candidate comprehends instructions, acts independently, and multitasks effectively.
 



  






 Key Responsibilities:
 



* Own daily work activities ensuring master data is accurately maintained in line with industry deadlines across several business areas.
* Execute daily tasks with a high level of accuracy and accountability
* Work within a tight-knit team of Product Master Data experts to ensure data flows via GDSN and support them in their daily work
* Root cause, identify and resolve any issues that are reported
* Work cross-functionally to ensure issues are resolved and business critical tasks are carried out in a timely manner.



  





**Your Profile:** 





 Qualifications:
 



* BSc or MSc in any discipline, with a preference for Science, Technology, Engineering and Mathematics (STEM) and Supply Chain Management degrees.
* Proven experience of identifying root causes, proposing and actioning solutions
* Very strong attention to detail
* Strong communication & comprehension skills, both verbally and written
* Ability to balance deadlines and own workload according to business priorities
* Fast learner and strong sense of ownership & accountability




 Technical Skills:
 



* Advanced in Microsoft Excel (Pivot tables etc.)
* Experience using SAP or similar
* Proficient in Power BI for reporting
* Proven analytical experience with multisource and large data sets.
* Business English required
* Swedish beneficial but not a requirement



**Interested?** 




 If you are up for the challenge and think it suits you, we'd like to hear from you. Apply today, the position might be filled before the application deadline.

title: Head, Commercial (dark stores)
description:
foodora is part of the Delivery Hero Group, the world’s pioneering local delivery platform, our mission is to deliver an amazing experience—fast, easy, and to your door. We operate in over 70+ countries worldwide. Headquartered in Berlin, Germany. Delivery Hero has been listed on the Frankfurt Stock Exchange since 2017 and is part of the MDAX stock market index.
   

  

**Job Description**
 foodora market continues to grow, develop and expand at an extremely fast pace! We are breaking new ground for the entire grocery trade through our darkstores business. In our darkstores we store thousands of products to ensure that our customers can do their weekly shopping and get it delivered to their door in less than 30mins after placing their order. Cool huh? foodora market is a game changer and we hope you want to join our exciting journey!
   

  

**What You Will (but Not Only) Do**
* The scope of the role is broad and covers everything from highly strategic decisions to very hands-on tasks such as assortment workshops, negotiating with suppliers and wholesalers to doing the monthly performance materials.
* You are responsible for foodora markets customer offering which means: what meets the customer when opening the app.
* In more details. you (and your team) are responsible for conversion, site performance overall, assortment, price, campaigns, average basket value, items per order, price levels balanced with profitability measures, promotions such as samplings and all other non-margin revenues, and our external collaborations.
* P&L responsibility for Commercial revenues & costs.
* Leading our category management and pricing strategies
* Plan, host and develop our annual talk-of-the-town “Supplier Day”
* You have an essential role in our Management team, contributing to the overall strategic direction, day-to-day decisions and ensuring the commercial aspect in all decisions made
* Coach, lead, set and track developments for the Commercial team.
* Collaborate with internal stakeholders from other functions such as the operations -, logistics -, marketing -, product and finance team and take actions to drive the business forward
* Collaborate with Delivery Hero’s regional and central teams as well as sister companies across Europe. Answering up to the regional team on how we perform on the local KPI scorecards, and set & follow up on action plans to reach the targets


**Qualifications**
* You know the grocery retail business inside and out either from the supplier or retailer side, preferably also from an e-com and/or q-com perspective.
* You are used to leading in an environment where you need to balance the needs and expectations of multiple departments and stakeholders.
* You have a university degree within business or economics or a long experience of work that have given you similar experience
* You have 7+ years experience in a managing role with budget ownership & team leadership
* Familiarity with category management and strategies
* You have experience from collaboration and stakeholder management in an international context
* You have professional knowledge of excel/google sheets and powerpoint and are confident on stage.
* You communicate fluently in Swedish and English both verbally and in writing


**Additional Information**
* Wellness allowance topped with the opportunity to participate in many work/life balance initiatives at foodora such as free group training at Sats & reduced price on massage.
* Employee discount at foodora (woho!) and awesome Friday breakfasts!
* Occupational pension, incl. premium exemption insurance, accident insurance and life insurance.
* foodora Learning - Monthly learning sessions about different topics, such as the q-commerce industry, negotiation techniques and project management combined with a structured onboarding and inspiring courses within our learning tool Sana
* Great deals at Benify (for e.g. gym membership, e-bikes hotels, audiobooks, streaming services, clothes, kitchen supplies… and so much more!
* Awesome AW’s and (pink) parties!

title: Head of Global Transport & Trade Compliance Operations
description:
**Company Description**
***Statistics show that women and underrepresented groups tend to apply to jobs only if they meet 100% of the qualifications. Sobi encourages you to change that statistic and apply. Rarely do candidates meet 100% of the qualifications. We look forward to your application!***
 At Sobi, the work we do every day redefines the standards of care and transform the lives of people living with rare diseases.
   

  

 As a diverse team of entrepreneurial people, we are passionate about our growth journey towards becoming a global leader, making a difference for rare disease patients, moving quickly and always challenging the status quo.
   

  

 We are committed to an inclusive, sustainable and flexible workplace that fosters growth and development.
   

  

**Job Description**
 The
 **Head of Transport & Trade Compliance (T&TC)** 
 is accountable for leading Sobi’s global transportation and trade compliance operations, ensuring reliable, compliant and efficient execution across day‑to‑day activities. The role safeguards patient supply, business continuity and regulatory compliance across transports of Drug Substance, Drug Product, Finished Goods, clinical materials etc, in line with GDP/GMP, customs, export control and international trade regulations.
   

  

 Beyond running stable operations, the role plays a key part in enabling change, driving continuous improvement and preparing the organisation and network for increasing complexity. This includes navigating ambiguity, finding pragmatic solutions for new products, markets and evolving regulatory requirements, and continuously strengthening ways of working in an increasingly complex global environment. The Head of T&TC is expected to challenge the status quo where needed, while maintaining high standards of compliance, service and operational stability.
   

  

 The role is a senior leadership position within the Delivery leadership team, with a strong focus on people leadership, capability building and developing a resilient organisation prepared for future growth. It is also a key capability role with potential to take on broader responsibilities as the Delivery organisation evolves.
   

  

 This is a hybrid position, located at Sobi’s headquarters in Stockholm, reporting directly to the Head of Delivery.
   

  

**Key Responsibilities**
* Lead Sobi’s global transport and trade compliance operations, ensuring reliable execution and continuity of patient supply.
* Ensure compliance with GDP/GMP, customs, export control and trade regulations, while enabling practical solutions in a changing global environment.
* Act as the escalation point for transport disruptions and compliance risks, balancing speed, stability and compliance.
* Drive performance management of logistics partners, improving service, quality, cost and compliance.
* Lead, coach and develop the global Transport & Trade Compliance team, building capability to manage increasing complexity.
* Identify risks and improvement opportunities, contributing operational insight to ongoing change and development initiatives.


**Qualifications**
**About you**
 You are a confident and pragmatic leader who thrives in complex, regulated environments. You combine deep operational understanding with the ability to see the bigger picture — balancing today’s execution with tomorrow’s needs. You enjoy leading people, building trust, and creating clarity in fast‑moving situations.
   

  

 You are energized by responsibility and take ownership naturally. You collaborate easily across functions, cultures, and external partners, and you communicate with credibility and calm — even under pressure. Most importantly, you are motivated by purpose and want your work to make a real difference for patients.
   

  

**Key Qualifications**
* Experience leading global transport and trade compliance operations in a regulated pharmaceutical or life science environment, with the ability to navigate ambiguity and challenge established ways of working.
* Solid understanding of GDP/GMP, customs, export control and international trade regulations, combined with a pragmatic, solution‑oriented approach.
* Experience operating in complex global supply chains, including temperature‑controlled pharmaceutical logistics and evolving product or market requirements.
* Proven people leadership experience, with the ability to build teams that can handle increasing complexity and change.
* Experience managing external logistics partners and driving performance through collaboration, data and continuous improvement.
* Strong analytical skills and the ability to drive continuous improvement
* Experience working with ERP/TMS systems (e.g. IFS or similar)
* Swedish language skills and experience with digitalisation or network optimisation are considered a plus.


 Here at Sobi we live by our core values: Care, Ambition, Urgency, Ownership and Partnership!
   

  

**Additional Information**
**How to apply**
 We have an ongoing selection process in this recruitment and ask you to send your application as soon as possible. Click apply and include your resume in English.
   

  

**Why Join Us?**
 Here At Sobi, Our Mission And Culture Get Us Excited To Come To Work Every Day, But Here Are a Few More Reasons To Join Our Team
   

  

* Emphasis on work/life balance
* Collaborative and team-oriented environment
* Opportunities for professional growth
* Diversity and Inclusion
* Competitive compensation for your work
* Making a positive impact to help ultra-rare disease patients who are in need of life saving treatments


 We are a global company with over 1,900 employees in more than 30 countries and are committed to the societies where we operate. With a deeply skilled management team directing our day-to-day wins, and a Board with a stellar track record, we’re ready to take on the world’s diseases, ailments and adversity. Our people believe they have the power to make a positive impact in others’ lives because that’s exactly what we do here. If you’re seeking a career that taps into your talents in a way that makes the world a better, healthier place, we just may have a job for you.
   

  

 We know our employees are our most valuable asset, and our culture conveys that. We offer a competitive benefits package, to support the health and happiness of our staff.
   

  

**Sobi Culture**
 At Sobi, we refuse to accept the status quo. This is because we have witnessed first-hand the challenges facing those affected by rare diseases and have used this knowledge to shape our business to find new ways of helping them.
   

  

 As a specialized biopharmaceutical company, we are dedicated to rare diseases. And we see this focus as a strength. By effectively turning our research into ground-breaking treatments, we help make medicine more accessible and open up more possibilities for patients and more opportunities for those caring for them. This has been our approach since day one, but we know we can’t change the world of rare diseases on our own. Accomplishing this requires strong partnerships with patients, partners and stakeholders across the entire value chain. Together, we define how our business can create solutions that serve the needs of those affected by rare diseases while facilitating sustainable growth.

title: Team Lead - System Engineering
description:
At Capgemini Engineering, the world leader in engineering services, we bring together a global team of engineers, scientists, and architects to help the world’s most innovative companies unleash their potential. From autonomous cars to life-saving robots, our digital and software technology experts think outside the box as they provide unique R&D and engineering services across all industries. Join us for a career full of opportunities. Where you can make a difference. Where no two days are the same.
   

  

 Har du passion för ledarskap, teambuilding och teknik och för att hjälpa andra att lyckas och utvecklas? Då är detta positionen för dig!
   

  

 Vi söker nu en Team Lead till Stockholm som kommer ha en kombinerad roll av teamansvar samt att bidra till ett tekniskt uppdrag/leverans.
   

  

 Som Team Lead ansvarar du för ett team av systemingenjörer som levererar mot våra kunder inom bland annat försvars- och fordons-industrin. Vi arbetar med System Engineering och utvecklar och hanterar krav på Produkter och System inom tex styrsystem, mha Modell Baserad Design metodik. Vi arbetar även inom el och elektronik på produkt- & systemnivå, till exempel som el- eller elektronikingenjör inom energiförsörjning, elmaskiner & styrsystem. Du kommer att samarbeta med vårt Talent-team, för att kunna rekrytera rätt talanger. Du utvecklar teamet för att säkerställa en sund laganda, du drivs av att se människor växa i deras roll som konsult och utveckla vår verksamhet. Mentorskap, coaching och kontinuerlig feedback är väsentligt för denna roll.
   

  

 Rollen kommer att vara en kombination av teamansvar och att vara konsult i vår leverans. Det här är ett utmärkt tillfälle för dig som vill ta ditt första eller andra steg in i en ledarroll i en högpresterande teammiljö.
   

  

*I den här rollen kommer du ha en nyckelroll i att:*
* Rekrytera nya teammedlemmar, samt ha personalansvar för ditt team
* Stödja Engineering Unit Manager med personalhantering, affärstillväxt med mera
* Hantera och stödja karriärutveckling för ditt team
* Bidra till tekniska lösningar/försäljningsaktiviteter av nya projekt
* Arbeta med tekniska kundförslag
* Vara en del av teknisk leverans/uppdrag mot våra uppdragsgivare


 Din profil
   

  

* Högskoleexamen inom elektronik, data, produktutveckling alt el/mekatronik eller närliggande område
* Bra ledarskap & samarbetsförmåga
* Tidigare erfarenhet som Systemingenjör / System ledare / Scrum Master / Projektledare / Teamledare / Teknisk ledare
* Flytande i svenska & engelska i tal och skrift


**Vad Vi Tror Kommer Få Dig Att Trivas**
* En kombinerad roll mellan tekniska projekt, samt team lead ansvar
* En kultur där vi värnar om din kompetensutveckling
* Att jobba för ett globalt företag där våra projektlösningar både är lokala och globala


 Ansökan
   

  

 Som en del av vår rekryteringsprocess kommer identitetskontroll att genomföras genom granskning av ditt ID-kort, och bakgrundskontroller kan utföras vid behov.
   

  

 Capgemini is an AI-powered global business and technology transformation partner, delivering tangible business value. We imagine the future of organizations and make it real with AI, technology and people. With our strong heritage of nearly 60 years, we are a responsible and diverse group of 420,000 team members in more than 50 countries. We deliver end-to-end services and solutions with our deep industry expertise and strong partner ecosystem, leveraging our capabilities across strategy, technology, design, engineering and business operations. The Group reported 2024 global revenues of €22.1 billion.

title: Global Project Manager
description:
**About Wolt**
 At Wolt, we create technology that brings joy, simplicity and earnings to the neighborhoods of the world. In 2014 we started with delivery of restaurant food. Now we’re building the delivery of (almost) everything and you’ll find us in over 500 cities in 30 countries around the world. In 2022 we joined forces with DoorDash and together we keep on dreaming big and expanding across the globe.
   

  

 Working at Wolt isn’t always easy, but it’s definitely exciting. Here you’ll learn more, build more, and ship more than in most other companies. You’ll be challenged a lot, but also have a lot of fun on the way. So, if you’re a self-starter with drive and entrepreneurial spirit, this could be the ride of your life.
   

  

 Are you ready to drive complex physical security projects across borders and build resilient, future-proof security systems? We're looking for an experienced and dynamic Global Project Manager to join our team in a pivotal role, covering all Wolt regions.
   

  

**What You'll Be Doing**
* Advance and implement comprehensive regional security strategies across multiple countries.
* Conduct regular site reviews, and risk assessments to identify vulnerabilities and recommend mitigations.
* Manage and oversee the installation, maintenance, and upgrades of physical security systems across the regions.
* Ensure full compliance with legal, regulatory, and privacy requirements, both local and international.
* Act as a key liaison, collaborating across departments and hierarchies to align security needs with business priorities.
* Innovate and streamline operational tools and systems to improve security efficiency and service delivery.


**Our humble expectations**
* 7+ years of relevant experience in physical security systems management within an international organization or environment.
* Strong expertise in security systems access control, CCTV and alarm systems).
* Fluency in English (written and spoken); other languages are a plus.
* Ability to operate under pressure and manage complex security projects.
* Excellent interpersonal and communication skills with a solution-oriented mindset.
* Knowledge of local and global security regulations and standards.
* Proactive, reliable, and ready to support on all matters regarding security systems.
* Experience with managing large scale projects and delivering technical knowhow to the project team.
* Experience in working with at least two of the following systems AltaOpen, Ava, Lenel, Milestone.


 Why join us
   

  

 💙 You’ll get to work in a company culture where we take ownership beyond the obvious, do common things uncommonly well, we think big but stay humble, do right by people, we treat others kindly and justly, recognise that if we don’t learn, we won’t stay still but fall behind.
   

  

 🙌 Join us for an exciting journey! The compensation will be a combination of monthly base pay and equity. The latter makes it exceptionally easy to be excited about our company growing and doing well, as you'll own a piece of the pie.
   

  

**Compensation**
 The successful candidate’s starting pay will fall within the pay range listed below and is determined based on factors such as the candidate’s skills, experience and qualifications. Please note, that the starting pay may vary if the candidate does not fully meet the expectations as outlined in this job posting.
   

  

 The salary below is the monthly salary range for this position.
   

  

 To learn more about our benefits, please do not hesitate to bring this up with our Talent Acquisition Partner!
   

  

 Latvia Pay Range:
   

  

 €4.120 - €6.180 EUR
   

  

 Lithuania Pay Range:
   

  

 €4.906 - €7.360 EUR
   

  

 Our Commitment to Diversity and Inclusion
   

  

 We’re committed to growing and empowering a more inclusive community within our company, industry, and cities. That’s why we hire and cultivate diverse teams of people from all backgrounds, experiences, and perspectives. We believe that true innovation happens when everyone has room at the table and the tools, resources, and opportunity to excel.

title: Research assistant
description:
**The Department of Biochemistry and Biophysics.** 
 SciLifeLab (SciLifeLab) is a national center for molecular biosciences with a focus on health and environmental research. The center combines frontline technical expertise with advanced knowledge of translational medicine and molecular bioscience. SciLifeLab is a national resource hosted by Karolinska Institutet, KTH Royal Institute of Technology, Stockholm University and Uppsala University. The center also collaborates with several other universities.
   

  

 The employment will be placed at the Department of biochemistry and biophysics, at Stockholm university.
   

  

 More information about us, please visit: the Department of Biochemistry and Biophysics.
   

  

 Main responsibilities
   

  

 The Pollak Dorocic lab at Stockholm University studies the diversity of neuromodulatory neurons and circuits in the brain. We use a combination of molecular and systems neuroscience tools to visualize, record, and manipulate neural populations to understand their function and contribution to behaviour.
   

  

 Main responsibilities are hands-on laboratory work, including molecular biology, microscopy, neural recordings and animal model behavioral assays. The candidate will be involved in different research projects in the lab, and will also have the possibility to develop their own project.
   

  

 Successful candidates will have the opportunity to work in a vibrant and highly collaborative environment with state-of-the-art infrastructure.
   

  

 Read more
   

  

 Qualification requirements
   

  

 We are looking for someone with previous experience in neuroscience research, including rodent stereotaxic surgery, behavioral assays, in vivo electrophysiological or calcium recordings, and computational analysis.
   

  

**About The Employment**
 This is a full-time, term-limited employment of 12 months’ duration. Stockholm University applies individual salary setting. Start date during the second half of 2026.
   

  

 We offer
   

  

 With us, you will experience the dynamic interaction between higher education and research that makes Stockholm University an exciting and creative environment. You will work in an international environment and get favourable conditions. The university is located in the National City Park with good transport links to the city.
   

  

 Stockholm University strives to be a workplace free from discrimination and with equal opportunities for all.
   

  

 Contact
   

  

 Further information about the position can be obtained from Iskra Pollak Dorocic, iskra.pollak-dorocic@dbb.su.se.
   

  

 Application
   

  

 Apply for the position at Stockholm University’s recruitment system. It is the responsibility of the applicant to ensure that the application is complete and that it is submitted before the deadline.
   

  

 Only include the cover letter and CV in your application. Bring copies of grades and certificates if you are called to an interview.
   

  

 The instructions for applicants are available at: How to apply for a position.

title: Group Treasury Quantitative Analyst | SEB, Stockholm
description:
Curious about what happens when a client deposits money with SEB? Join SEB as a Group Treasury Quantitative Analyst and gain deep insights into liquidity transformation, shaping our balance sheet structure and future financial stability.
   

  

 At SEB, we recognise that the world of finance is evolving at an extraordinary speed. That's why we're committed to driving towards a more sustainable future for everyone — people, businesses, and society. Since we welcomed our first customer in 1856, we have been a catalyst for positive change by providing reliable advice and capital to people with ideas for the future. If you are ready to make your mark on our industry and positively shape the world, you will thrive at SEB.
   

  

**About the role:**
 As a Group Treasury Quantitative Analyst, you will gain deep insights into liquidity transformation and how it shapes SEB's balance sheet structure. You'll join Balance Sheet Management (BSM) to quantify Balance Sheet Risks and make projections to fund SEB Group for the best risk vs. reward. This involves building models for structural market and liquidity risks from deposits taking activities. You will explore financial data, make balance projections, and drive improvement initiatives within Liquidity Risk management and Interest Rate Risk in the Banking Book (IRRBB).
   

  

**To thrive in this role, we believe you have:**
* At least 10 years of experience in a relevant quantitative finance or treasury position.
* An advanced degree (Master's or equivalent) in statistics, mathematical finance, or a similar field, or equivalent skills acquired through work experience or extracurricular activities.
* Demonstrated ability to build complex models and perform exceptionally well in data-intensive and intricate environments.
* A strong analytical mindset and professional fluency in English, both written and oral.
* A dedicated team player with a profound curiosity for how banks function and operate.


**What we offer:**
* Extensive training and learning opportunities
* Long-term stability and growth as a company
* International working environment


**Do you want to be part of SEB?**
 It is our fundamental belief that inclusion and diversity is crucial for our future success. We strive to have an inclusive, value-driven culture where employees feel valued, respected, and involved irrespective of who they are, what they believe or where they come from.
   

  

 Since we select candidates continuously, feel free to send in your application today via the link in this ad, but no later than
 **2026-03-18.** 
 If you have questions about the position, please contact
 **Gustav Walås** 
 , Head of Balance Sheet Management, GFM, gustav.walas@seb.se, note that we will not process applications via email.
   

  

 Please be aware that our final candidates undergo background checks, a process that includes for example identity control, verification of qualifications, credit checks, company engagements and history of crime. In some cases, we also apply random drug checks.
   

  

 Learn more about working at SEB on our Career website or through our Career podcasts.
   

  

 https://sebgroup.com/career
   

  

 https://bit.ly/SEBcareer
   

  

 SEB Sweden has a redeployment responsibility, which is why this position might be covered by internal redeployment.

title: AI/ML Engineer
description:
***Redpill Linpro är ett företag med djupa rötter inom Open Source och arbetar med de senaste tekniska lösningarna inom våra kärnområden DevOps, API & Integration, Analytics och Cloud. Vi har några av Nordens tyngsta experter inom Azure, AWS, Apache Kafka, Linux, Kubernetes, PostgreSQL, för att nämna några exempel. Vi arbetar med åtaganden och utvecklingsprojekt i många olika branscher i Sverige, Norge och Danmark.***
 Vi letar nu efter en AI/ML Engineer till vår Data & Analytics-enhet. I de här rollen kommer du att spela en central roll i att fortsätta utveckla vårt erbjudande. Du kommer att arbeta med kunder som redan kommit en bit på sin resa mot att bli datadrivna och hjälpa dem att utveckla moderna lösningar med hjälp av de senaste teknikerna inom AI och Analytics. Som AI/ML Engineer förstår du att grunden i varje sådan satsning är en säker tillgång till relevant data och det är din roll som konsult att hjälpa våra kunder på den resan. Du måste därför njuta av att både vara hands-on, använda en whiteboard och utmana kunderna, dig själv och det team du arbetar i.
   

  

**Vi letar efter dig som har erfarenhet av följande:**
* Flera års erfarenhet av AI/ML Engineer eller motsvarande
* Relevant akademisk utbildning
* Kunskap om maskininlärningsmodeller och generativ AI
* Erfarenhet av Databricks, Spark och Kafka
* Erfarenhet av utveckling i Python samt SQL
* Erfarenhet av visualiseringsverktyg som Power BI, Qlik eller Tableau
* Erfarenhet av uppsättning av cloud-infrastruktur i AWS, GCP eller Azure
* Erfarenhet av Infrastructure as Code, med verktyg som t ex Terraform
* Erfarenhet av automatiserade releaseprocesser, DevOps och CI / CD
* Erfarenhet av datamodellering (strukturerad och ostrukturerad data)
* Erfarenhet av agil utveckling
* Erfarenhet av konsultrollen
* Flytande i svenska och engelska


 Vill du vara med på resan? Sök tjänsten nu – vi ser fram emot att höra från dig!
   

  

 Redpill Linpro is the leading provider of Open Source services and products in the Nordics. We are located in seven offices with a mission to share knowledge and best practices for a more open society.
   

  

 We are passionate about open solutions, open business models, and an open society where we work together for maximum benefit to everyone. We have got some really skilled co-workers here at Redpill Linpro, particularly within our main areas: API management, development, IT operations, data analytics, customer experience, cloud, DevOps, and automation.
   

  

**May the Source be with you!**

title: Bid Manager – Technical Consulting & Sales | ALTEN Stockholm
description:
Are you a Bid Manager who can turn complex engineering into winning consulting deals? At ALTEN, this is not a traditional bid role focused on administration. You will play a key role in shaping offers, working closely with Sales and Presales to turn advanced engineering capabilities into clear, competitive, and winning proposals.
 



  





 If you understand the consulting business model and can translate technical solutions into business value, we want to hear from you.
 



  





**Your role** 




 You will lead complex bids end-to-end, from early qualification to final submission, ensuring each opportunity is strategically positioned to win.
 



  





 You act as the link between technical and commercial teams, transforming engineering expertise into clear, executive-level value propositions for clients.
 



  





**What you will do** 




 • Lead the full bid lifecycle, from qualification to submission
 



 • Shape and position offers to maximize win probability
 



 • Translate complex software and hardware solutions into clear business value
 



 • Support large-scale transformation initiatives by aligning ALTEN’s capabilities with client needs
 



 • Analyze market trends, competitor positioning, and pricing strategies
 



 • Coordinate Sales, Presales, Legal, and Finance to deliver strong and profitable offers
 



  





**Who you are** 




 • You understand how consulting services are structured, priced, and sold
 



 • You see bids as strategic business opportunities, not just documentation
 



 • You are structured, proactive, and comfortable taking ownership
 



 • You thrive in fast-paced environments with multiple stakeholders
 



 • You can simplify complex technical input into clear, impactful messaging
 



  





**Requirements** 




 • Master’s degree in a relevant field
 



 • 5+ years of experience in bid management, consulting, or technical sales
 



 • Experience from a consulting services company is highly valued
 



 • Strong understanding of software and/or hardware engineering services
 



 • Fluent in English; Swedish is a strong advantage
 



  





**About ALTEN & why join us** 




 ALTEN is one of Europe’s leading engineering and IT consulting companies, with a strong presence in Sweden and internationally. We support some of the most innovative companies with advanced engineering solutions.
 



  





 Joining ALTEN means:
 



 • A key role with direct impact on business growth
 



 • High level of ownership and influence on major deals
 



 • An international environment with strong local presence
 



 • A clear bonus structure and strong career opportunities
 



  





**Apply now** 




 Take the opportunity to shape winning offers and contribute to ALTEN’s continued growth.

title: Category Manager IT/OT, Solna
description:
Är du en strategisk inköpsspecialist med djup förståelse för IT och OT – och vill vara med och forma PostNords framtida tekniska landskap?
   

  

 Vi söker nu en Category Manager inom IT, med inriktning mot OT, som vill driva utvecklingen av våra globala inköpsstrategier och säkerställa att PostNords operativa och digitala miljöer fortsatt är robusta, innovativa och kostnadseffektiva.
   

  

 Detta är en möjlighet för dig som vill kombinera affärsmässighet, teknikförståelse och strategiskt inköp och som vill bidra till PostNords fortsatta digitalisering och operativa utveckling.
   

  

**Om rollen**
 Som Category Manager IT/OT är du en del av Group Sourcing och ansvarar för att utveckla och driva inköpsstrategier inom området IT och Operational Technology. Du genomför upphandlingar och arbetar nära verksamheten, IT-organisationen och våra produktionsmiljöer för att säkerställa att inköpen stödjer både drift, säkerhet, innovation och kostnadseffektivitet.
   

  

 Du rapporterar till Head of Category IT och samarbetar med interna stakeholders i Sverige, Danmark, Norge och Finland, samt med externa strategiska leverantörer på både lokal och global nivå.
   

  

**Dina huvudsakliga ansvarsområden**
**Strategi & kategoriutveckling**
* Leda och utveckla strategier för inköp inom IT och OT, inklusive mjukvara, hårdvara och automationslösningar.
* Analysera spend, marknadstrender, riskområden och innovationsmöjligheter.
* Identifiera kostnadsdrivare och standardiseringsmöjligheter.
* Säkerställa att kategorins processer utvecklas i linje med PostNords inköpsmodell och mål.


**Marknadsexpertis & leverantörsrelationer**
* Utveckla och vårda leverantörsbasen inom IT och OT.
* Utvärdera befintliga partners och identifiera nya leverantörer som kan bidra med innovation, säkerhet och skalbarhet.
* Integrera hållbarhet, digitalisering och cybersäkerhet i leverantörsstrategin.


**Förhandling & avtalshantering**
* Ansvara för förhandlingar och avtal inom IT/OT‑området.
* Säkerställa avtal som stödjer driftssäkerhet, efterlevnad och riskminimering.
* Genomföra upphandlingar enligt LUF/LOU/FSVD


**Intressent- och behovshantering**
* Arbeta nära IT, produktion, säkerhet, logistik och terminalverksamheter för att förstå behov och driva gemensamma initiativ.
* Representera kategorin i beslutsforum och stödja verksamheten i strategiska teknikval.
* Leda och koordinera sourcinginitiativ och implementeringsaktiviteter tillsammans med relevanta funktioner.


**Resultatansvar**
* Säkerställa att kategorins finansiella och operativa mål nås.
* Driva kostnadsoptimering, riskreducering och kontinuerliga förbättringar.


 ,
   

  

 Vi Söker Dig Som Har En Stark Kombination Av Strategiskt Tänkande, Teknisk Förståelse Och Förmåga Att Driva Affärsresultat. För Att Lyckas i Rollen Tror Vi Att Du Har
   

  

* Flerårig erfarenhet av strategiskt inköp, kategoriutveckling eller sourcing inom IT, teknik, industri eller motsvarande område
* God förståelse för IT‑infrastruktur, system, tjänster och gärna även produktions IT‑miljöer.
* Erfarenhet av avancerade förhandlingar och leverantörsstyrning.
* Relevant akademisk examen.
* Meriterande med erfarenhet av LUF/LOU/FSVD, komplexa tekniska projekt eller arbete i större koncerner.


 Du är analytisk, strukturerad och affärsdriven och har förmågan att kombinera teknik, affär och relationer på ett sätt som skapar värde för hela PostNord.
   

  

**Du ska må bra**
 Vi Är Ett Modernt Kundorienterat Företag Som Tänker Lika Mycket På Våra Anställda Som På Våra Kunder. Hos Oss Erbjuds Du Ett Omväxlande Arbete i En Internationell Miljö Med Ständigt Nya Utmaningar Och Snabba Beslut. Förutom Schyssta Anställningsvillkor Enligt Kollektivavtal Erbjuder Vi
   

  

* Flexibel hybrid arbetsplats. Se filmen om vårt kontor
* Goda utvecklings- och karriärmöjligheter
* Goda försäkrings- och tjänstepensionsvillkor
* Friskvårdsbidrag samt möjlighet att träna på Arkens gym och simhall
* Personalförmåner genom PostNord Plus – vår personalstiftelse


**Om oss**
 PostNords vision är att vara favoritleverantören i Norden. Vi har ett viktigt samhällsuppdrag och säkerställer kommunikations- och logistiklösningar för företag och privatpersoner. Vi är i en ständig transformation och arbetar konsekvent med att utveckla både vårt erbjudande och våra medarbetare, allt för att nå vår vision samt göra vardagen enklare och mer hållbar för alla som bor och arbetar i Norden. Vi är PostNord – ett fantastiskt företag att jobba och växa med. Läs mer på group.postnord.com
   

  

**Ansökan**
 Är du den vi söker? Välkommen med din ansökan så snart som möjligt då vi intervjuar löpande. Har du frågor om tjänsten är du välkommen att kontakta ansvarig rekryterare Emina Delkic på emina.delkic@postnord.com.

title: LIA/Internship within SEO
description:
****About WPP Media****
 WPP is the trusted growth partner for the world’s leading brands. With exceptional talent, trusted data and intelligence, and world-class partnerships – all united by our pioneering agentic marketing platform, WPP Open – we help clients navigate change, capture opportunity, and deliver transformational growth.
   

  

 WPP Media is WPP's AI-driven media operating unit, bringing together media, data, and partnerships to deliver creative personalisation at scale. Connected through WPP Open and powered by Open Intelligence, clients see exactly where, how, and why their media investment is working.
   

  

 For more information, visit wppmedia.com.
   

  

****About WPP Media****
 WPP is the creative transformation company. We use the power of creativity to build better futures for our people, planet, clients and communities. For more information, visit wpp.com.
   

  

 WPP Media is WPP’s global media collective. In a world where media is everywhere and in everything, we bring the best platform, people, and partners together to create limitless opportunities for growth. For more information, visit wppmedia.com.
   

  

 At WPP Media, We Believe In The Power Of Our Culture And Our People. It’s What Elevates Us To Deliver Exceptional Experiences For Both Our Clients And Each Other. In This Role It Will Be Critical To Embrace WPP & WPP Media’s Shared Core Values:
   

  

* Be Extraordinary by Leading Collectively to Inspire transformational Creativity.
* Create an Open environment by Balancing People and Client Experiences by Cultivating Trust.
* Lead Optimistically by Championing Growth and Development to Mobilize the Enterprise.


**Ongoing intake, but limited spots available**
 As an intern in our SEO & Content Engineering team at WPP Media, you’ll be working with a focus on SEO.
   

  

 You’ll get to experience all aspects of SEO - from strategy and implementation to follow-up and analysis. You'll work actively in our client teams, helping some of the largest companies in Sweden and around the world. We believe in continuous learning and skill development, and we encourage sharing knowledge within the team.
   

  

 Our office is located on Birger Jarlsgatan in Stockholm, and for the right candidate, there may be opportunities to continue your career with us after the internship.
   

  

**Who are you?**
 We believe you’re a student in media, web, marketing, finance, or someone with relevant work experience and a strong interest in digital marketing. You’re eager to learn, enjoy problem-solving, and can contribute new ideas. You’re proactive and can work both independently and collaboratively with others. Experience with SEO tools isn’t required, but being comfortable in Microsoft Excel is a plus.
   

  

**To join our team as an intern, you need to:**
* Be available for at least 8 weeks
* Do your internship through a school or the Swedish Public Employment Service (Arbetsförmedlingen)
* Be available full-time during standard office hours
* Be fluent in Swedish and comfortable using English
* Be available full-time to work on-site at our Stockholm office


 Please note: this internship is unpaid.
   

  

**Interested?**
 Apply with your CV and include a short note about why you’d like to intern with us. Be sure to include the period you're applying for.
   

  

**Contact**
 If you have questions about the SEO internship, contact Sandra Mellqvist, Senior Consultant & Intern Manager, SEO & Content Engineering. Due to GDPR, we do not accept applications via email – please submit your application through Greenhouse.
   

  

 Please read our Privacy Notice for more information on how we process the information you provide.

title: Säkerhetsskyddskonsult
description:
Rejlers är
 **ett av Nordens ledande teknikkonsultbolag** 
 med verksamhet inom energi, industri, infrastruktur och samhällsutveckling. Med över
 **3000 experter** 
 hjälper vi företag, myndigheter och organisationer att möta morgondagens samhällsutmaningar.
   

  

 Nu stärker vi vårt affärsområde
 **Samhällssäkerhet** 
 och söker fler kollegor som vill vara med och utveckla vårt erbjudande inom
 **säkerhetsskydd** 
 . Genom vår kombination av
 **djup kompetens, ständig omvärldsbevakning och praktisk erfarenhet av nya hot och risker** 
 hjälper vi våra kunder att stärka sitt säkerhetsarbete och sin motståndskraft.
   

  

 Vi Söker Nu Fler Kollegor, Juniora Som Seniora, Med Erfarenheter Av Säkerhetsarbete Inom Ett Eller Flera Av Följande Områden
   

  

* Informationssäkerhet i IT-system
* Personalsäkerhet med säkerhetsprövningar
* Fysisk säkerhet med systemuppbyggnad
* Beredskapsplanering med analys, plan och uppföljande verksamhet


**ABOUT THE JOB** 
 I rollen som
 **Senior säkerhetsskyddskonsult** 
 blir du en del av vårt team inom
 **samhällssäkerhet** 
 , där vi arbetar med att stötta organisationer i att
 **identifiera, hantera och förebygga säkerhetshot** 
 .
   

  

 Teamet jobbar idag med informationssäkerhet, personalsäkerhet, fysisk säkerhet och beredskapsplanering.
   

  

 Som konsult hos oss arbetar du nära våra kunder, som främst består av
 **myndigheter, kommuner och företag inom samhällsviktig verksamhet** 
 . Uppdragen varierar i både omfattning och komplexitet, vilket ger dig möjlighet att arbeta både strategiskt och operativt.
   

  

**I Rollen Kan Du Exempelvis Arbeta Med Att**
* genomföra säkerhetsskyddsanalyser och riskbedömningar
* stötta organisationer i implementering av säkerhetsskyddsåtgärder
* agera rådgivare eller säkerhetssamordnare i större projekt
* bidra till att utveckla metoder och arbetssätt inom säkerhetsskydd


 Du blir en del av ett erfaret konsultteam där kunskapsutbyte och lärande är en naturlig del av vardagen.
   

  

**WHO ARE YOU?** 
 Vi söker dig som har
 **arbetslivserfarenhet inom säkerhetsskydd** 
 , gärna från
 **både offentlig och privat sektor** 
 . Du har relevant utbildning inom området och erfarenhet av att arbeta med exempelvis säkerhetsskyddsanalyser, säkerhetssamordning och/eller implementering av säkerhetsskyddsåtgärder.
   

  

 För den här rollen krävs att du:
   

  

 ✔ Kommunicerar obehindrat på svenska och engelska, i tal och skrift
   

  

 ✔ Är svensk medborgare då tjänsten kräver godkänd säkerhetsprövning
   

  

 Det är meriterande om du har fördjupade kunskaper inom något av följande områden:
   

  

* Informationssäkerhet
* Personalsäkerhet
* Fysisk säkerhet
* Säkerhetsskyddad upphandling


 Som person är du
 **prestigelös, flexibel och samarbetsorienterad** 
 . Du trivs i dialog med både kollegor och kunder och har förmågan att
 **arbeta självständigt, ta initiativ och driva arbetet framåt** 
 . Eftersom vissa uppdrag genomförs hos våra kunder runt om i landet är det också viktigt att du
 **är beredd att resa i tjänsten** 
 .
   

  

**WHAT YOU GET** 
 Hos oss blir du en del av en
 **kompetent och engagerad konsultgrupp med cirka 20 kollegor** 
 , många med erfarenhet från
 **försvarssektorn och andra samhällsviktiga verksamheter** 
 . Ledarskapet präglas av
 **förtroende, uppdragstaktik och en stark gemenskap** 
 , där varje konsult har möjlighet att påverka både sina uppdrag och sin egen utveckling.
   

  

 På Rejlers får du möjlighet att
 **arbeta i teknikens framkant och bidra till utvecklingen av ett säkrare och mer resilient samhälle** 
 . Vi erbjuder en
 **modern arbetsplats där lärande, samarbete och utveckling står i centrum** 
 .
   

  

 Vår vision sammanfattas i
 **Home, Learning och Minds** 
 . Det innebär att vi vill vara ett
 **hem för våra medarbetare och kunder** 
 , byggt på tillit och öppenhet. Genom
 **kontinuerligt lärande och kunskapsdelning** 
 utvecklar och inspirerar vi varandra, våra kunder och våra partners – samtidigt som vi tillsammans tar oss an
 **dagens och morgondagens mest komplexa samhällsutmaningar** 
 .
   

  

**#HomeOfTheLearningMinds**
*Som ett kvalitativt led i våra rekryteringsprocesser kommer en bakgrundskontroll genomföras på slutkandidaten.*
 Vissa Roller Kräver Även Annan Kontroll, Såsom T.ex. Säkerhetsprövning, Innan Uppdrag Hos Vissa Kunder - Om Det Är Aktuellt För Tjänsten Du Sökt Så Kommer Du Att Få Mer Information Kring Det i Ett Senare Skede. Ytterligare Information Hittar Du Även Här
   

  

 Behandling av personuppgifter | Rejlers
   

  

*Vi granskar ansökningar löpande och tjänsten kan komma att bli tillsatt innan sista ansökningsdag.*

title: Team Manager/Project Manager
description:
**Nu vill vi stärka vårt team med ytterligare en Team manager!**
**På landets snabbast växande** 
 kontor söker vi en kollega som ska leda och utveckla en grupp härliga systemutvecklare samt bidra med kunskap inom intressanta projekt inom industriell automation. Detta är en nyckelroll som ska bidra till vår plan att vara en champion inom automationsbranschen!
   

  

*" Efter att ha vunnit förtroende att leverera våra tjänster genom flera stora och viktiga avtal ser vi det som en naturlig följd att förstärka vår organisation med nya kollegor. Med denna tillsättning ser vi till att säkerställa ett bra ledarskap för att kunna fokusera på ytterligare utveckling. Du kommer att ingå i en grupp med fullt fokus på att leverera det bästa för våra kunder. På Sveriges snabbast växande kontor tävlar vi om nya anledningar att fira tillsammans med våra kollegor. Välkommen att bli en del av INIT Swedens Stockholmskontor"- Roger Löfgren, Business Unit Manager*
**Om Rollen**
 Rollen är en kombinationsroll, vilket innebär att du ofta också är en projektledare, men kan också vara t ex en Affärsutvecklare.
   

  

* Du kommer att ingå i ett engagerat och inspirerande team som tillsammans leder kontoret i Stockholm. Du har personalansvar (rekrytering av systemutvecklare, medarbetar- och utvecklingssamtal och lönejusteringar) för dina medarbetare likväl som ansvar för ditt projekts framfart. Dina ledaregenskaper kommer komma väl till hands i rollen som team manager som i projektledningen.
* Du säkerställer att vi levererar det vi lovat i rätt tid, med rätt kvalitetskrav till rätt budget. Storleken på projekten kan vara allt ifrån mindre eftermarknadsprojekt till stora projekt för prestigefulla kunder.
* I rollen ser du även potentiella affärsmöjligheter och deltar i anbudsarbetet. Du kommer att kunna bidra till att generera nya projekt, och är även delaktig i merförsäljningen i befintliga projekt. Vi arbetar med ett projektverktyg som följer vår projektprocess - som du använder som hjälpmedel i din vardag.


**Vi Söker Dig**
 Som är en erfaren projektledare/teamledare inom
 **automation** 
 och har ett
 **naturligt engagemang** 
 för att motivera, engagera samt säkerställa kvaliteten i varje steg av processen. Du har god förmåga att skapa förtroendegivande relationer, både internt och externt. Du är en naturlig ledare som är prestigelös och ödmjuk i ditt sätt.
   

  

* Kvalifikationer bör inkludera +5års arbete med automation
* Erfarenhet av att leda projekt inom installation, infrastruktur eller industri där tjänst, material, underkonsulter och/eller underentreprenörer kombineras
* Erfarenhet av att leda entreprenadprojekt, både löpande och fasta avtal/projektleveranser inom privat eller offentlig sektor
* Erfarenhet av budget - och resultatansvar
* Vana att arbeta mot uppsatta KPI:er
* Branschkännedom
* Tidigare erfarenhet av personalledning är högt meriterande.
* Talar och skriver svenska flytande, samt är svensk medborgare beroende på att flera av uppdragen hos våra kunder omfattas av säkerhetsskyddslagen.


**Du blir en del av**
 En väldigt spännande resa när vi tillsammans med ett tiotal andra bolag nu bygger
 **Nordens bästa automationshus!**
 Vi har en ambition att samla de bästa företagen och människorna inom industriell IT och automation med en total omsättning på 1,5 MDR kr och ca 1000 anställda i Danmark, Sverige, Norge, Serbien och Spanien, som är specialister inom industriell IT, automation, IOT och OT cybersäkerhet.
   

  

* Ett företag med en tydlig och kontrollerad tillväxtstrategi
* Goda karriärmöjligheter med professionella, inspirerande och innovativa miljöer med plats för nörderi och professionell utveckling.
* Ett företag som stöttar ditt specialområde


 Vi tror att bra arbete kommer från glada och motiverade människor. Det är därför vi lägger lika mycket kraft på att ge dig en rolig, säker och inkluderande arbetsmiljö som vi gör på att utveckla dina professionella förmågor. Oavsett om det handlar om sociala evenemang eller balans mellan arbete och privatliv gör vi allt vi kan för att göra Init till en positiv del av ditt liv.
   

  

**Placeringsort;** 
 Stockholm, Liljeholmen på vårt nya kontor
   

  

**Start:** 
 Enligt överenskommelse
   

  

**Närmaste ledare:** 
 Roger Löfgren, Business Unit manager
   

  

**Vad händer efter att du har skickat in din ansökan?**
 Antalet steg i ansökningsprocessen kan variera beroende på vilken tjänst du söker, men vi försöker alltid hålla processen så kort och koncis som möjligt för att skapa en bra ansökningsupplevelse. Vanligtvis kan du förvänta dig två intervjuer innan referenstagning.
   

  

 Vi läser ansökningar och träffar kandidater löpande så skicka in din ansökan redan idag! Vid frågor, kontakta mig, Rekryterare, Sara Rensfeldt Romin, på sara.rensfeldt@initgroup.com
   

  

 Vi ser fram emot din ansökan! #Init2winit
   

  

 Init hjälper verksamheter att göra styrning, övervakning och systemintegration smartare. Vi kombinerar affär, organisation och teknik så att allt hänger ihop – i praktiken.
   

  

 Vi är vana vid miljöer med höga krav på effektivitet, tillgänglighet och säkerhet. Därför tar vi ansvar hela vägen: från idé och design till programmering, driftsättning och löpande underhåll. Då kan kunden fokusera på sin kärnverksamhet.
   

  

 Vi har gjort det här i mer än 30 år, i fastigheter, industri och infrastruktur – alltid med samma mål: att göra komplexa system enklare att använda.

title: DevOps Engineer - Tieto Caretech (m/f/d)
description:
**Job Description**
**At Tieto Caretech you’ll get a chance to provide patients and end users with product offerings that help them in their life, reinventing the world for good. If that sounds interesting, please keep on reading.**
**About Us**
 Tieto Caretech is modernizing the Nordic health and social care sector with modular, open, and interoperable software. We develop and deliver high quality software solutions and services that enable healthcare and welfare professionals to do their work even better and with higher efficiency - have more time to care.
   

  

 We are a global team of professionals working on providing products and services to different sectors from intensive care to laboratory and social care. In addition to working closely with our customers, we also have our own products that we build and develop for various fields in society. From enabling healthcare professionals to spend more valuable time with their patients to providing data for professional athletes to achieve even better results, we make all that possible. With us you can build technology for the good of society and make your mark on the health and wellbeing of people in need.
   

  

**About The Role**
 We work in an agile, multinational organization that develops cutting-edge products and services for both public and private sectors across the Nordic countries. Our teams are committed to creating, supporting, and delivering modern solutions used by healthcare and social care professionals throughout the region.
   

  

 We are now looking for DevOps Engineers for our Lifecare Open Platform and Clinical Core Solution Operations team to work on our OpenEHR products. Your daily work consists of delivering and deploying our solutions to our customers and providing support in case of needed.
   

  

**In this role you will**
* Participate in delivering and deploying our products to different customers
* Participate in supporting our customers in case needed


**We are looking for someone with**
* Strong understanding of containerization technologies (e.g., Docker, Kubernetes)
* Experience with infrastructure automation and orchestration tools (e.g., Terraform, Ansible, Helm)
* Knowledge of CI/CD pipelines and version control systems (e.g., Git, Jenkins, GitLab, Azure DevOps)
* Proficiency in scripting and automation (e.g., Python, Bash, PowerShell)
* Familiarity with monitoring, logging, and alerting tools (e.g., Prometheus, Grafana, ELK stack, OpenTelemetry)


**We expect you to have a**
* Strong understanding of customer needs and expectations
* Ability to troubleshoot and resolve issues across infrastructure and application layers
* Ability to document and present complex solutions to stakeholders
* Capability to analyze complex systems with an end-to-end approach


**It would be beneficial if you also have**
* Proven experience working directly with customers
* SW development experience


**As a person you are**
* Eager to develop yourself and learn
* Good team player, attention to detail and you have ability to work well with others
* Able to support and consulting clients or internal teams


**What’s in it for you?**
 With us you will be part of the important and meaningful work of developing healthcare for all of us. You have an important mission to make a difference that really matters. In addition, as a company we offer you professional growth, open and friendly culture, and an outstanding work-life balance! We believe that our organizational culture is an important part of enabling you to be successful. We provide a flexible hybrid work model as part of our culture and way of working.
   

  

 Finally, we also believe in curiosity and learning as a lifestyle. We want to encourage you to keep up that curiosity and deepen your knowledge on topics you’re interested in. For example, we host a ‘Keep Learning Week’ twice a year, a week where everyone at Tieto has the possibility to join different training courses in a truly global setting.
   

  

**Could you be our new colleague?**
 We look forward to hearing from you! We will fill the position as soon as we find the right person, so we encourage you to apply today. If you have any questions regarding this role, please contact the hiring manager Timo Heikkilä (timo.j.heikkila@tieto.com)
   

  

*We perform background checks on all final candidates.*
*Tieto declines calls from recruitment companies.*
 Additional Information
   

  

 At Tieto, we believe in the power of diversity, equity, and inclusion. We encourage applicants of all backgrounds, genders (m/f/d), and walks of life to join our team, as we believe that this fosters an inspiring workplace and fuels innovation. Our commitment to openness, trust, and diversity is at the heart of our mission to create digital futures that benefit businesses, societies, and humanity.
   

  

 Diversity, equity and inclusion (tietoevry.com)

title: Cloud Solution Architects Manager
description:
**Overview**
 With over 17,000 employees worldwide, the mission of the Customer Experience & Success (CE&S) organization is to empower customers to accelerate business value through differentiated customer experiences that leverage Microsoft’s products and services, ignited by our people and culture. Come join CE&S and help us build a future where customers achieve their business outcomes faster with technology that does more.
   

  

 In the Customer Success team, we are looking for people with a passion for delivering customer success. As a Cloud Solution Architect Manager, you will run a team who enable customers to achieve their outcomes, based on their investments in Microsoft technology. Leveraging your leading technical team’s expertise, you will drive the team to ensure customers get value from their MS investments. Microsoft aspires to help our customers architect and deploy first class cloud infrastructure solutions in Azure to be AI Ready by applying scalable best practices in the cloud with Cloud Adoption Framework (CAF) and Well-Architected Framework (WAF). Azure is the most comprehensive, innovative, and flexible cloud platform today and Microsoft is hiring professionals that will drive customer cloud adoption for AI Innovation within the most important companies in the market.
   

  

**Responsibilities**
* You will build and grow teams through talent management, diversity and inclusion, coaching, and career development. Inspire and foster a culture of customer-centricity and consumption, adoption and through collaboration with peers and extended teams and develop a team that incorporates a mix of expertise across key workloads and lead them through changes using the Demonstrated Capability Framework
* You will lead your team to deliver positive Customer Satisfaction, and coach them to become trusted advisors to customers and partners, by leveraging Azure Infrastructure services to operate AI solutions at scale and enable defined Customer Success Plan outcomes. Coach the team to provide feedback & insights from customers/partners back to the relevant MS teams including Product Groups, to enable continuous improvement and AI Innovation.
* As the cloud-consumption leader, owning the business and results you will be the vocal leader and customer champion with both local and corporate leadership to elevate cloud adoption issues and accelerate resolution.
* You will empower your team to win and accelerate application and AI innovation projects to production, including guidance on architectural design, required services to operate AI solutions at scale, including Infrastructure, Data, Networking, Security, and Management, technical recommendations, blocker escalations and technical resource orchestration.
* You will engage with the area’s most strategic customers as executive sponsor to accelerate digital and AI transformation and help customers build microservices architectures with scalable databases to capture innovation. Accountability to ensure revenue and business metric are achieved.
* You will lead your team to utilize “Well-Architected” framework, such as high levels of performance, security, scalability, maintainability, and appropriate reusability and reliability with an emphasis on Networking, Zonal/multi-region, and Security and partner with all required Microsoft stakeholders in the subsidiary to land and drive CSA role clarity, account coverage, project assignment and effective cross-team collaboration.
* You will foster a continuous self-learning culture and drive technical intensity, to enable customers to realize desired business outcomes, including innovation. Model impactful community engagement, sharing learnings and best practices, and coach inclusivity.


**Qualifications**
**Required Qualifications (RQs)** 
  

  

* Bachelor's Degree in Computer Science, Information Technology, Engineering, Business, Liberal Arts, or related field AND significant years experience in cloud/infrastructure technologies, information technology (IT) consulting/support, systems administration, network operations, software development/support, technology solutions, practice development, architecture, and/or consulting
+ OR equivalent experience

* several years people management experience, including managing consultant practice managers, technical sales managers, and/or technical architect managers
* Fluency in Swedish language


**Preferred Qualifications (PQs)**
* Bachelor's Degree in Computer Science, Information Technology, Engineering, Business, Liberal Arts, or related field AND decade plus years experience in cloud/infrastructure technologies, information technology (IT) consulting/support, systems administration, network operations, software development/support, technology solutions, practice development, architecture, and/or consulting
+ OR Master's Degree in Computer Science, Information Technology, Engineering, Business, or related field AND significant experience in cloud/infrastructure technologies, technology solutions, practice development, architecture, and/or consulting
+ OR equivalent experience


* several years experience working in a customer-facing role (e.g., internal and/or external)
* several years experience leading technical projects, teams, or functions
+ Technical Certification in Cloud (e.g., Azure, Amazon Web Services, Google, security certifications)

* several years people management experience, including managing consultant practice managers, technical sales managers, and/or technical architect managers


 This position will be open for a minimum of 5 days, with applications accepted on an ongoing basis until the position is filled.
   

  

 Microsoft is an equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to age, ancestry, citizenship, color, family or medical care leave, gender identity or expression, genetic information, immigration status, marital status, medical condition, national origin, physical or mental disability, political affiliation, protected veteran or military status, race, ethnicity, religion, sex (including pregnancy), sexual orientation, or any other characteristic protected by applicable local laws, regulations and ordinances. If you need assistance with religious accommodations and/or a reasonable accommodation due to a disability during the application process, read more about
 **requesting accommodations.**

title: Production Engineer - Pharma
description:
At Capgemini Engineering, the world leader in engineering services, we bring together a global team of engineers, scientists, and architects to help the world’s most innovative companies unleash their potential. From autonomous cars to life-saving robots, our digital and software technology experts think outside the box as they provide unique R&D and engineering services across all industries. Join us for a career full of opportunities. Where you can make a difference. Where no two days are the same.
   

  

**Your Role**
 We are looking for a Production Engineer for our pharmaceutical projects in Stockholm
   

  

*In this role you will play a key role in:*
* Operating independently within your area of expertise and lead others while driving competence development.
* Investigating deviations, support production technical issues, and contribute to improvement projects.
* Manage tasks such as issuing recipes and batch records, coordinating installations, or producing plant layouts and detailed designs.
* Lead or participate in subprojects related to process engineering, automation, or plant design.
* Ensure quality and documentation compliance according to given guidelines.


**Your Profile**
* Master’s or bachelor’s degree in science (or equivalent).
* At least 3 years of experience working in the pharmaceutical industry
* Process development and technology with experience from Process technology, Automation, Facility project planning
* Strong technical proficiency in Process Engineering, Automation, Plant Design, or Project Management.
* Ability to work autonomously and deliver results based on guidelines while assuring quality.
* Experience in leading small projects or subprojects and collaborating across functions such as plant engineers and design engineers.
* Good communication and problem-solving skills to support production and improvement initiatives.


**What We Believe You Will Enjoy About Working Here**
* We recognize the significance of flexible work arrangements to provide support. Be it remote work, or flexible work hours, you will get an environment to maintain healthy work life balance.
* At the heart of our mission is your career growth. Our array of career growth programs and diverse professions are crafted to support you in exploring a world of opportunities.
* We support your personal development and offer an education library with more than 250 000 (!) courses and educations and certifications.


**Application**
 As part of our recruitment process, identity verification or background checks may occasionally be conducted.
   

  

 Valid work permit for Sweden is required.
   

  

 Capgemini is a global business and technology transformation partner, helping organizations to accelerate their dual transition to a digital and sustainable world, while creating tangible impact for enterprises and society. It is a responsible and diverse group of 340,000 team members in more than 50 countries. With its strong over 55-year heritage, Capgemini is trusted by its clients to unlock the value of technology to address the entire breadth of their business needs. It delivers end-to-end services and solutions leveraging strengths from strategy and design to engineering, all fueled by its market leading capabilities in AI, generative AI, cloud and data, combined with its deep industry expertise and partner ecosystem.

title: Data Scientist, Subscriptions
description:
The Subscriptions Mission drives the growth and evolution of the Spotify business, ensuring we deliver compelling value to hundreds of millions of subscribers worldwide. We are focused on building the right set of offerings, plans, and value propositions that attract new subscribers and deepen engagement with existing ones.
   

  

 You'll join the Portfolio Data Science team, part of Subscriptions Product Insights. The Portfolio Focus Area is responsible for evolving and adapting Spotify's existing subscription plans to drive subscriber growth and engagement, as well as creating new revenue streams through add-ons and new value propositions. This work requires coordination across the entire Subscriptions Mission and multiple focus areas, with ownership and oversight from the Portfolio leadership team.
   

  

 As a Data Scientist on this team, you'll help the Portfolio team make informed decisions about how to grow and diversify Spotify's paid offerings. You'll analyze subscriber behavior, measure the impact of plan changes and new propositions, and surface insights that shape our strategy. Your work will directly influence how the Spotify experience evolves for users around the world.
   

  

 What You'll Do
   

  

* Analyze subscriber behavior and engagement patterns to identify opportunities for plan evolution and new value propositions
* Design and analyze experiments to measure the impact of changes to subscription plans and add-on offerings
* Define and maintain success metrics for Portfolio initiatives, ensuring alignment across cross-functional partners
* Build and maintain dashboards and reporting to monitor the health and performance of subscription plans and new revenue streams
* Perform exploratory analyses that uncover growth levers and inform the Portfolio roadmap
* Collaborate with Product, Engineering, Design, User Research and other Data Science teams across the Subscriptions Mission
* Communicate findings and recommendations clearly to both technical and non-technical audiences, including senior leadership
* Use AI-powered tools (e.g., Claude Code, Codex) to accelerate analysis and workflows


 Who You Are
   

  

* You have 3+ years of experience in data science or analytics, ideally at a consumer tech or subscription-based company
* You hold a degree in Statistics, Economics, Computer Science, Mathematics, or a related quantitative field (or equivalent experience)
* You are proficient in SQL and Python and comfortable working with large-scale datasets
* You have experience designing and analyzing A/B tests, with a solid understanding of statistical methods
* You are a clear communicator who can translate complex analyses into actionable recommendations
* You are curious, proactive, and comfortable navigating ambiguity in a fast-moving business
* You enjoy collaborating across teams and are comfortable working in environments that require coordination across multiple stakeholders and workstreams


 Where You'll Be
   

  

* This role is based in New York, Stockholm or London.
* We offer you the flexibility to work where you work best! There will be some in person meetings, but still allows for flexibility to work from home.
