title: Senior Windows Client & System Engineer
description:
Nordnet is a leading pan-Nordic digital platform for savings and investments. Ever since our start in 1996, our purpose has been to democratize savings and investments. Through passion, simplicity, and transparency, we challenge traditional ways in the financial industry, and give private savers access to the same information, tools and services as professionals. Our ambition is to inspire, encourage and enable people to take their savings to the next level, no matter who they are.  

  

**The role in brief**


We are looking for a Senior Windows Client & System Engineer to join our team of six. You will be responsible for maintaining a secure, high-performing technical environment for all Nordnet employees.


In this role, you will take ownership of our client platform, ensuring it meets the stringent security and compliance standards of the financial industry. Your primary focus is the proactive lifecycle management of Windows and mobile devices using Microsoft Intune to maintain a modern, secure, and efficient digital workplace.

 **Key Responsibilities**

* Windows & Mobile Management: Lead the operation, maintenance, and technical evolution of our Windows and mobile (iOS/Android) platforms.
* Build and manage advanced configuration profiles and automated deployment policies within Microsoft Intune.
* Identity & Security: Configure Conditional Access in Microsoft Entra, ensuring a deep understanding of policy logic and its impact on security.
* Patch Management: Automate the deployment of software and Windows Updates to ensure a secure and compliant environment.
* Ensure all managed devices meet the regulatory and security requirements of the financial industry.

 **Your Profile**


We are looking for a structured, solution-oriented professional with a methodical approach to technical challenges. You have a "can-do" attitude and a genuine interest in understanding system dependencies and security details.


Qualifications and Experience:

* Several years of experience in client management within a large-scale enterprise environment.
* Windows & Intune Specialist: In-depth technical expertise in the Windows platform and extensive experience with Microsoft Intune.
* Security Mindset: Strong understanding of Conditional Access, Microsoft Entra, and identity-driven security.
* Automation Pro: Advanced skills in PowerShell for system management and lifecycle automation.
* Mobile Fleet Management: Experience securing and managing mobile devices in a professional setting.
* Language: Professional fluency in both Swedish and English, written and spoken.

 **About Nordnetters**  

  

We value experimentation and continuous improvement. With an impartial approach, we welcome new ideas to boost our progress. Sharing knowledge and learning from our setbacks is part of the game. We have the courage to ask questions and admit that we do not have the answer, all the time. We believe that straightforward and transparent communication build trust and inclusion.


Culture is built and cared for, each day by everyone. We’re proud of ours. Having a flat organization where anyone can talk to anyone creates a warm and friendly atmosphere worth protecting. We believe in a culture where every effort counts and where everyone is being recognized. A culture embracing our core values – passion, simplicity and transparency on all levels, no matter who you are or what you do.  

  

**What we offer**  

  

We offer you the opportunity to work in a great team within a friendly Nordic environment with a strong focus on innovation, product development and technology. We are proud of the friendly and humble environment and commit to our goals as a team.


We are 800 employees in Stockholm, Oslo, Helsinki and Copenhagen.


You can also visit us on: www.nordnet.se, www.nordnet.no, www.nordnet.dk and www.nordnet.fi.  

  

**Application**  

  

If you have questions, please contact Head of IT-Support, Patrik Axelsson, patrik.axelsson@nordnet.se


Apply today– we will start interviewing immediately. Please note that we do only accept applications through our recruitment system, hence no email applications will be considered.


We want to inform you that Nordnet conducts mandatory credit and background checks, as well as drug testing as a part of our recruitment process.


We look forward to receiving your application!

title: Global Banking & Markets – Software Engineer (C++) – Associate – Stockholm
description:
**Job Description**
**Goldman Sachs**
 The Goldman Sachs Group, Inc. is a leading global investment banking, securities and investment management firm that provides a wide range of financial services to a substantial and diversified client base that includes corporations, financial institutions, governments, and individuals. Founded in 1869, the firm is headquartered in New York and maintains offices in all major financial centers around the world.
   

  

**Divisional Overview**
 Global Banking and Markets division provides financing, advisory, risk distribution, and hedging services for institutional and corporate clients. Within Global Banking and Markets, our equities professionals focus on delivering ideas, execution, and liquidity to a wide array of clients trading the entire range of equity products, including physical shares, ETFs, synthetic shares, baskets, options, and other derivatives. Our engineers play important roles in several areas within the division. Some engineers build automated trading platforms and algorithms for the firm and its clients, taking an active part in the increasing shift from voice to electronic trading. Others sit on trading desks, creating cutting-edge derivative pricing models, and developing empirical models to provide insight into market behavior. Still others develop systems and processes that assist with making the entire trade life cycle more seamless.
   

  

**GSET Engineering**
 Goldman Sachs Electronic Trading (GSET) offers a best-in-class comprehensive suite of trading strategies to help clients achieve their trading objectives and navigate a variety of market conditions leveraging historical analyses, quantitative models, and real-time market information. These strategies are built on a highly distributed, scalable, low latency trading platform, which processes billions of transactions daily in the low microsecond range, with minimal jitter. Our engineers are responsible for designing, building, and maintaining this trading platform as well as the strategies running on the platform, in a collaborative environment. The software our engineers write is of a very high standard: it is client and market facing with real time SLAs and can have high financial and regulatory impact. Some engineers focus on implementing the platform, its surrounding ecosystem and order management workflows, writing highly performant code and optimizing its interaction with the hardware and infrastructure it is executing on. Others work on building the trading models, signals and algorithmic logic which constitute the trading strategies.
   

  

**Who Are We Looking For**
 We are looking for strong engineers in the GSET Platform and Connectivity space. The team is part of building one of GSET’s major trading platforms which processes billions of dollars’ worth of trades every day. The team writes highly performant code using best-in-class software development processes and seeks to continuously improve all aspects of the system. We push our own boundaries and challenge the status quo in others. Our main goal is to create performant, scalable and resilient trading systems through best-in-class software change management. We want technical people that communicate well and have a passion for efficiency and automation, continually looking for ways to improve. As part of this effort, they will be expected to collaborate with colleagues across the world.
   

  

**Your Impact**
 Working as a member of the GSET Platform and Connectivity team, you will play an active role in ensuring our trading applications remain best-in-class. You will be responsible for maintaining and developing the platform used for accessing equity exchanges and other trading platforms around the world, implementing solutions related to order routing, dissemination of market information, controls evaluation, and the transaction platform that supports these functions – all while maintaining our low latency performance profile. Together with the team you will tackle complex software engineering problems in an environment where we collaborate across business functions to deliver new solutions every day.
   

  

 The ideal candidate brings customer service excellency in their approach and have a solid understanding of distributed software systems.
   

  

**Basic Qualifications**
* Bachelor's or Master's degree in Computer Science or related field
* Productive in programming in an object-oriented language
* C++ programming experience
* Ability to work in a fast-paced, dynamic environment
* Ability to challenge status quo and automate manual tasks
* Ability to drive complex change to completion in collaboration and independently
* Knowledge of basic networking concepts, topologies, and technologies
* Strong analytical and problem-solving skills
* Strong communication skills and ability to work in a team


**Preferred Qualifications**
* Experience of low-latency systems, including messaging and network protocols
* Working knowledge of order management systems
* Knowledge of financial markets


**About Goldman Sachs**
 At Goldman Sachs, we commit our people, capital and ideas to help our clients, shareholders and the communities we serve to grow. Founded in 1869, we are a leading global investment banking, securities and investment management firm. Headquartered in New York, we maintain offices around the world.
   

  

 We believe who you are makes you better at what you do. We're committed to fostering and advancing diversity and inclusion in our own workplace and beyond by ensuring every individual within our firm has a number of opportunities to grow professionally and personally, from our training and development opportunities and firmwide networks to benefits, wellness and personal finance offerings and mindfulness programs. Learn more about our culture, benefits, and people at GS.com/careers.
   

  

 We’re committed to finding reasonable accommodations for candidates with special needs or disabilities during our recruiting process. Learn more: https://www.goldmansachs.com/careers/footer/disability-statement.html
   

  

 © The Goldman Sachs Group, Inc., 2023. All rights reserved.
   

  

 Goldman Sachs is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, sex, national origin, age, veterans status, disability, or any other characteristic protected by applicable law.

title: Real Time 3D Developer (Unreal / Unity)
description:
Ready for something different? Are you an experienced and passionate developer looking for the next challenge in your career? Piktiv is on a mission to help our clients leverage game technology even more than is being done today. We are looking for the right people to join our team and bring this vision to life. If you would like to combine the benefits of working as a consultant with the benefits of working in a product company, we at Piktiv would like to hear from you!
   

  

**Who We Are**
 We are a progressive consultancy company that provides smart solutions for our clients. Our work spans from heavy industry systems, healthcare applications, retail and fintech systems, to computer games. We pride ourselves on our commitment to excellence and our ability to turn complex challenges into streamlined solutions.
   

  

 What sets us apart is that Piktiv brings in client projects to work on in-house by providing highly skilled fullstack teams instead of individual consultants. We are currently looking to expand our team with developers who have experience working with modern game engines to help us deliver solutions for our clients across multiple industries.
   

  

**Who You Are**
 You have several years of experience in software development and a strong background working with modern game engines such as Unreal Engine or Unity. You enjoy solving complex problems and building robust systems.
   

  

 You are both technically strong and pragmatic in how you approach solutions. You are comfortable taking ownership of technical decisions and leading development efforts when needed.
   

  

 You communicate clearly with both technical and non‑technical stakeholders and enjoy collaborating with multidisciplinary teams. Experience working with industrial companies is a strong plus.
   

  

 You seek an engaging work environment that allows you to work with challenging technical problems, a high degree of personal freedom and responsibility.
   

  

**What You Will Do**
 As part of our team, you will work on projects that leverage game engines to solve complex challenges for our clients.
   

  

 Responsibilities include:
   

  

* Designing and developing applications using game engines such as Unreal Engine or Unity
* Building real‑time 3D applications such as simulations, visualization tools, digital twins, and other interactive systems
* Collaborating with designers, engineers, and client stakeholders to deliver high‑quality solutions
* Taking a leading role in projects where game technology is a core component
* Contributing to architectural decisions and ensuring scalable, maintainable implementations
* Helping drive projects forward from concept through delivery


**Qualifications**
* Several years of professional software development experience
* Strong experience with Unreal Engine or Unity
* Professional proficiency in English


**Desirable Skills**
* Experience building simulation systems, digital twins, or visualization tools
* Experience working within manufacturing or automotive industries.


**What We Offer**
 At Piktiv, we believe in the importance of open communication and creating a collaborative workplace where each individual can thrive. We work hard to cultivate a culture of support and learning with an emphasis on a healthy work-life balance. We offer a competitive salary as well as several perks and benefits. Rather than offer a standardized plan across the company, we like to tailor our offer to your personal preferences in person. The position is full-time, 40 hours per week.

title: Backend Engineer, Security
description:
Security engineers at Spotify protect the security of Spotify’s platform and of our 700+ million users. We are looking for an experienced engineer to join us in securing the most important engineering initiatives at Spotify.
   

  

 We are looking for a backend software engineer to join our security compliance engineering team. We are hiring engineers who are enthusiastic about security and compliance, and who enjoy transforming large, complex problems into actionable, scalable, and reliable software solutions.
   

  

 What You'll Do
   

  

* Design products, services, and infrastructure that support and strengthen our technical security Governance, Risk, and Compliance (GRC) strategy
* Build infrastructure and tooling that apply regulatory requirements and security framework controls across our distributed environment
* Help drive scalable and consistent engineering practices that address security, risk, and compliance requirements


 Who You Are
   

  

* You are an experienced software engineer with development experience in an object-oriented programming language such as Java, and you are comfortable with data engineering
* You have experience writing distributed, high-volume services and know how to deploy and operate them in production
* You have a deep understanding of system design, data structures, and algorithms
* You care about quality and you know what it means to ship high quality code
* You have hands-on experience implementing security controls and safeguards in software systems, working within cybersecurity and security GRC frameworks
* You love working in an environment where you frequently discuss technical requirements and iterate quickly
* You are passionate about information security governance, risk and complianceYou have excellent written and verbal communication skills, including extensive experience meeting and presenting to senior leaders and non-technical stakeholders


 Where You'll Be
   

  

* This role is based in London or Stockholm
* We offer you the flexibility to work where you work best! There will be some in person meetings, but still allows for flexibility to work from home.


 Spotify is an equal opportunity employer. You are welcome at Spotify for who you are, no matter where you come from, what you look like, or what’s playing in your headphones. Our platform is for everyone, and so is our workplace. The more voices we have represented and amplified in our business, the more we will all thrive, contribute, and be forward-thinking! So bring us your personal experience, your perspectives, and your background. It’s in our differences that we will find the power to keep revolutionizing the way the world listens.
   

  

 At Spotify, we are passionate about inclusivity and making sure our entire recruitment process is accessible to everyone. We have ways to request reasonable accommodations during the interview process and help assist in what you need. If you need accommodations at any stage of the application or interview process, please let us know - we’re here to support you in any way we can.
   

  

 Spotify transformed music listening forever when we launched in 2008. Our mission is to unlock the potential of human creativity by giving a million creative artists the opportunity to live off their art and billions of fans the chance to enjoy and be passionate about these creators. Everything we do is driven by our love for music and podcasting. Today, we are the world’s most popular audio streaming subscription service.

title: Junior Research Engineer
description:
Join Varex Imaging, a global leader in X-ray imaging solutions, as we drive innovation in medical, industrial, and security imaging worldwide. With our cutting-edge products, advanced software, and a presence in over 20 locations worldwide, we're developing the industry forward at full speed.
 



  





 We are looking for a new member at our Swedish location to expand our talented and highly motivated Image quality Research Engineer team, and can offer you a great opportunity to broaden your knowledge and skills around X-ray detectors for the position of
 **Junior Research Engineer.** 
 In search we have a curious individual with a broad range of interests. Since image quality lies at the intersection of physics, electronics, mathematics and software, a generalist problem solver with an open mind would be ideal. A pragmatic individual with a "how hard can it be?" attitude.
 



  





 This is a full-time role offered in a hybrid format, which means few days’ office presence.
 



  





 Varex Careers
 



  





**What you'll be doing:** 




 • Create and optimize algorithms for image processing and correction
 



 • Design, perform and analysis of experiments for evaluating and improving image quality of our X-Ray detectors
 



 • Develop software tools for development of X-Ray detectors
 



  





**What we are looking for:** 




 • Preference will be given to applicants who have recently completed a Master's degree in Engineering Physics. Applicants from related fields, such as applied mathematics, are also encouraged to apply.
 



 • Some programming experiences. Python is strongly preferred, but other languages like MATLAB are also suitable
 



 • A solid understanding of the fundamentals of physics and communication in English between different stakeholders is essential
 



  





**Nice to have:** 




 • Interest/experience with electronics
 



 • Systems programming experience (Rust, C or C++)
 



 • Medical or other imaging experience
 



  





**What we offer:** 




 • A unique opportunity to grow as an expert in the X-ray imaging field with a team in which every person makes a difference
 



 • An international working environment
 



 • Individual training and development opportunities
 



 • Local benefits/perks
 



  





**Next Steps** 




 If you're interested and your background aligns with this role, our Talent Acquisition team will reach out to you. Following confirmation of a match, you'll have an exciting opportunity to meet with the hiring manager. This meeting will provide valuable insights into our company and allow you to ask any questions you may have. Ready to shape the future of X-ray imaging and make an impact in industry?
 



  





 Please submit your application through our recruitment portal on Workday ( Varex Careers )as soon as possible. We are actively interviewing candidates and will move forward quickly with the best fit for the team.
 



  





 Please note that you must press save before submitting your CV.
 



  





 Read more about us: https://www.vareximaging.com/photon-counting/
 



  





 Varex Imaging, headquartered in Salt Lake City, USA, is a leading innovator, developer, and manufacturer of X-ray imaging component solutions, which includes X-ray tubes, digital flat panel detectors, software, and other key components of X-ray imaging systems. With a 70+ year history of successful innovation, you will find our components in numerous medical, industrial, and security imaging applications worldwide.
 



  





 Across the world, our more than 2,200 employees are committed to support our mission making the invisible visible.
 



  





 All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, sex, color, religion, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, protected veteran status, or on the basis of disability.

title: E2E testledare
description:
**Professional Galaxy** 
 är ett IT- och teknikkonsultbolag som tillhandahåller högspecialiserad kompetens inom IT, mjukvaruutveckling, SAP, inköp, elektronik och mekanisk konstruktion. Vi samarbetar med erfarna seniora experter och levererar strategiskt värdeskapande kompetens till några av Sveriges mest komplexa och analytiskt krävande projekt. Vårt fokus ligger alltid på hög kvalitet, professionalism och tydliga, mätbara resultat.
   

  

 Vi söker nu en
 **E2E testledare** 
 hos vår kund.
   

  

**Om uppdraget**
 Säkerställa att förutsättningarna för testarbetet är på plats, dvs testplan, testdata är skapat, att integrationspunkterna är uppe, att inblandade team är redo mm. Vidare ska konsulten se till att alla tester blir genomförda enligt plan av alla olika intressenter och produktområden. Resultatet ska dokumenteras och eventuella avvikelser ska hanteras på lämpligt sätt.
   

  

**Krav**
 Kompetensnivå 4 (9-12 år).
   

  

 Minst 5 års erfarenhet av att arbeta agilt
   

  

 Minst 5 års erfarenhet av testledning
   

  

 Minst 5 års erfarenhet av manuell testning
   

  

 Minst 1 års erfarenhet av test mot PEGA-system
   

  

 Minst 5 års erfarenhet av att läsa komplexa krav och tillämpa dessa på testfall
   

  

 Minst 3 års Erfarenhet av JIRA, Confluence, X-ray
   

  

 Kunna hänvisa till minst 3 projekt där alla delar varit inblandade: Erfarenhet av alla led i testkedjan (enhetstest, systemtest, integrationstest, E2E-test), Erfarenhet av teknisk testning, erfarenhet av prestandatest, Erfarenhet av komplexa miljöer med många system, testdata, testfall samt underhåll av dessa, Erfarenhet av testautomatisering.
   

  

 Flytande på svenska i både tal och skrift.
   

  

**Bör-krav**
 Arbetat på myndighet.
   

  

 Erfarenhet av Play Wright.
   

  

 Erfarenhet av syntetisk testdata.
   

  

 Erfarenhet av Postman.
   

  

 Erfarenhet av arbete i datasystem kopplade till arbetsmarknadsinsatser för arbetslösa.
   

  

 Att utifrån flödesbeskrivningar kunna skapa relevanta tester e2e, dvs flöden som behöver testas, happy path såväl som viktiga sidoflöden.
   

  

**Övrig information**
 Placeringsort: Stockholm
   

  

 Arbetsmodell: Distansarbete upp till 49%
   

  

 Uppdragsperiod: 2026-04-24 - 2027-04-23 + 1 år
   

  

**Sista ansökningsdag:** 
 2026-03-29
   

  

 Är du rätt person för uppdraget, eller vill du rekommendera en stark kandidat? Tveka inte att kontakta oss.
   

  

 Ansök gärna redan idag, urval och intervjuer sker löpande
   

  

 Ansök genom att bifoga ditt CV (vi vill inte ha något personligt brev) och besvara urvalsfrågorna. Frågorna utgår från kravprofilen för den här rekryteringen och dina svar ligger till grund för det urval som sker i processen. Att besvara frågorna är en förutsättning för att din ansökan ska anses komplett.
   

  

 Vänligen notera att krav och eventuella meriter ska vara uppfyllda vid ansökningstillfället.

title: Senior Systems Engineer - Usability and Validation
description:
**We don’t just build technology. We build hope for everyone dealing with cancer.**
 At Elekta, you will make a difference. We are seeking a
 **Senior** 
**System Engineer – Usability and Design Validation** 
 for our team developing the Leksell Gamma Knife® within the Neuro Solutions Business Line. We are leaders in precision radiation therapy. Guaranteed precision spares healthy tissue to help protect the mind and the person.
   

  

 The Leksell Gamma Knife® consists of mechanical systems and software user interfaces used by clinicians, operators, patients, and service personnel. The Control System for Leksell Gamma Knife® manages accurate and safe delivery of radiation treatment to the patient. It spans from the operator’s user interface to the subsystems that drive the mechatronic components. It controls operator workflows, treatment targeting, imaging, safety functions and communication. The Leksell Gamma Knife® also includes a desktop integrated treatment planning system, Leksell GammaPlan, with large GUI desktop applications.
   

  

 You’ll work hands-on with both new development and maintenance of a safety-critical device in a regulated medtech environment. Your work will directly contribute to helping medical professionals deliver personalized and precise cancer treatments — while being part of a team that supports each other and collaborates closely across disciplines.
   

  

**What You’ll Work On**
 You’ll join a cross-functional engineering team and work closely with software, mechanical and verification engineers, product manager, safety engineer, technical writers, and application specialists. You will also work closely with the other usability and validation specialists within Neuro solutions. We value collaboration, open communication, and a supportive environment where everyone’s ideas are heard. From a usability engineering and design validation perspective, your work will focus on developing a device that is safe and effective for the intended users, uses and use environments. Your work will have a direct impact on patient treatment and the long-term evolution of the Leksell Gamma Knife®.
   

  

 As a
 **System engineer within Usability and Design Validation** 
 you will:
   

  

* Manage usability engineering and the Design validation activities, mainly for the Leksell Gamma Plan®, within the Neuro Solution Business Line, i.e. plan, execute, analyze, follow up, report and document. This includes, but is not limited to, workflow design, use scenario definition, formative and summative evaluations.
* Act as both Design Validation and Usability engineering specialist to guide and collaborate with colleagues such as safety engineers, developers, product managers, application specialists and validators.
* Participate in all R&D systems engineering activities with emphasis on Systems engineering tasks such as requirement gathering, understanding and defining user needs, concept modelling and safety analysis.
* Review process updates together with process owners and leaders to improve system engineering processes and ways of working, particularly those related to usability engineering and design validation.
* Follow the technical and clinical developments within the field (usability engineering, validation methods and related activities).
* Be aware of and take into consideration developments within the field, including new and changed standards.


**Who You Are**
 This is a senior technical role for an engineer with broad system understanding and focus on user needs, workflows and user experience.
   

  

 We think you’ll thrive if you:
   

  

* Have worked with both hardware and software product development. Experience within UX design is an advantage.
* Have documented knowledge and experience from Usability engineering work for medical devices and are familiar with applicable standards (i.e. IEC 62366-1).
* Are experienced with working from early concepts and user reviews through iterative prototyping and formative evaluation, summative evaluation and design validation.
* Appreciate cross-functional collaboration and want to work closely with specialists across software, mechanics, quality, product management and service.
* Want to work in a regulated medtech environment to do meaningful work that improves patient treatment.


 We also expect that you have
   

  

* B.Sc. or M.Sc. in Computer Science, Engineering Physics, Mechanical engineering or similar.
* Professional proficiency in English.
* Valid work permit for Sweden.


 You’ll have access to experienced usability and validation specialists, safety, and SW/HW engineers for onboarding and peer review of your plans and documentation. If you meet most—but not all—of the requirements, we still encourage you to apply. We know that strong candidates come from a variety of backgrounds.
   

  

**Hiring process**
 We are looking forward to hearing from you! Apply by submitting your application and résumé in English, via the “Apply” button. Please note that we do not accept applications by e-mail.
   

  

 We will review applications on a rolling basis, last day for applying is April 8th, 2026.
   

  

**Your Elekta contact**
 For questions, please contact Anna Sohlman, .

title: Senior mjukvaruutvecklare
description:
Vill du arbeta i ett globalt företag där du kan bidra till en mer hållbar livsmedelsproduktion? DeLaval är en ledande utvecklare, tillverkare och leverantör av lösningar för mjölkproducenter världen över. Som ett företag byggt på innovation arbetar vi ständigt för att hitta nya sätt att hjälpa våra kunder att göra mer med mindre.
   

  

 Nu söker ett av våra utvecklingsteam en erfaren mjukvaruutvecklare som vill vara med och driva den fortsatta utvecklingen av våra embedded-lösningar.
   

  

**Roll och ansvarsområden**
 I vårt team arbetar vi med produktutveckling från idé till färdig lösning, samtidigt som vi kontinuerligt vidareutvecklar och förbättrar befintliga system och komponenter. Vi utvecklar huvudsakligen embedded programvara i Java, där kraven specificeras i Polarion och även utgör grunden för våra automatiserade testfall.
   

  

 Vi följer en agil arbetsmetodik med 10 veckors increment och 2 veckors sprintar för att säkerställa regelbundna leveranser och ett tydligt tempo i utvecklingsarbetet. Samarbete är en central del av vår vardag, och vi arbetar tätt med både kravteam och integrationstest för att skapa robusta och välfungerande lösningar.
   

  

 För att kvalitetssäkra våra produkter har vi tillgång till egna testmiljöer, och vid behov utför vi också tester på riktiga robotsystem ute på gårdar. Detta säkerställer att våra lösningar fungerar i verkliga miljöer och uppfyller kundernas behov på bästa sätt.
   

  

**Kvalifikationer:**
**För att lyckas i denna roll tror vi att du har:**
* Du är en lagspelare som är stolt över ditt arbete och strävar efter hög kvalitet.
* Du har tillräcklig erfarenhet av mjukvaruutveckling för att kunna ta ett arkitektur ansvar.
* Du har en masterexamen inom datavetenskap eller motsvarande utbildning.
* Du trivs med att lösa komplexa problem och gillar nya utmaningar.
* Du behärskar svenska och engelska i tal och skrift.
* Du har erfarenhet av agilt arbetssätt.


**Det är också meriterande om du har:**
* Kunskap i Python, C eller C++ (används i äldre system).
* Erfarenhet av att skriva automatiserade testfall.
* Erfarenhet av verktyg som Jira, Confluence, Polarion, NetBeans och Git.
* Kunskap inom code quality assurance-verktyg.

title: Operations Quality Specialist
description:
**Who are Benifex? 💚💜** 




 We are a fast-moving technology company, and o
 **ne of the most successful providers of online reward and benefits solutions in the world** 
 . We believe that everyone deserves an exceptional experience at work, every day, and build
 **workplace technology** 
 that makes this happen. Benifex’s mission is to
 **build remarkable experiences that employees love** 
 . Today Benifex supports more than five million employees in over 3,000 organisations across more than 100 countries. To help us on our quest to be the best,
 **we need brilliant people** 
 on board and that’s where you come in. 🚀
 



  





**Why should you apply? 🌟** 



* Impact: Play a key role in ensuring platform stability, quality, and operational excellence across our platform.
* Ownership: Lead structured root‑cause analysis, risk and quality processes, and drive data‑informed improvements that have a measurable business impact.
* Problem-solving at scale: Work with complex incidents and deliver high‑quality second‑line support in a business‑critical environment.
* Collaboration: Work closely with engineering, operations, product, and commercial teams — translating technical insights into business‑ready actions.
* Top 10 Employer, Sunday Times Best Places to Work Awards
* Work for a profitable, fast-growing market leader in the online reward and benefits space



  





*Please note we are unable to offer visa sponsorship and require people to be based in Sweden for this position.* 




  





**Role Overview 🚀** 




 We’re looking for an
 **Operations Quality Specialist** 
 to help ensure consistent quality, operational stability, and risk management across our platform.
 



 In this role, you will
 **receive and evaluate incoming incidents** 
 , manage risk and quality cases, carry out structured
 **root‑cause analysis** 
 , and drive continuous improvement through data‑driven insights. You’ll document findings clearly, maintain ISO‑aligned practices, and translate technical outcomes into actionable recommendations for internal stakeholders.
 



 You’ll work closely with engineering, delivery, product, customer success (CSM/Marketplace) and finance to identify patterns, propose platform and process improvements, and maintain a strong feedback loop. This role is central to keeping our platform reliable, efficient, and aligned with best‑practice quality standards.
 



  





 You will report directly to the
 **Operations Quality Lead** 
 .
 



  





**Responsibilities 🤘** 




**Quality, Risk & Incident Management** 



* Receive and evaluate incidents, determining next steps and required stakeholders.
* Manage and document risk and quality cases, sharing key findings with internal teams.
* Conduct and document structured Root Cause Analysis (RCA) with clear issue statements, timelines, ownership, and preventive actions.
* Support major‑incident investigations and contribute to best‑practice incident management.
* Deliver high‑quality second‑line support and handle complex issues with professionalism.



  





**Analysis & Continuous Improvement** 



* Analyse operational and user‑experience issues to spot patterns and propose preventive improvements.
* Maintain and improve quality and risk processes focused on stability, efficiency, and customer experience.
* Drive preventive problem‑solving to reduce repeat incidents.
* Support goal‑setting and performance tracking for the Operations Quality function.



  





**Coaching, Communication & Lessons Learned** 



* Lead or contribute to lessons learned reviews and ensure actions are followed through.
* Translate technical details into clear, actionable insights for non‑technical stakeholders.
* Support quality assurance for planned communications like releases and maintenance notifications.
* Participate in internal and external stakeholder communication where required (e.g., CSM, vendors).



  





**Documentation & Compliance** 



* Ensure ISO‑compliant documentation and permission‑management practices.
* Maintain complete, searchable records for incidents, RCAs, and risk/quality cases.



  





**Experience** 




  





**What we're looking for 🔎** 



* Experience in quality, risk, operations, problem or incident management in a technical setting.
* Hands‑on experience conducting Root Cause Analysis (RCA) and supporting incident investigations.
* Experience working with engineering, delivery, product, or customer success teams.
* Familiarity with structured documentation.
* Experience with Jira or similar ticketing tools.



  





**Nice‑to‑have knowledge** 



* Understanding of Java script, APIs, or SFTP (sufficient to support investigations and follow technical flows).
* Familiarity with ISO standards or similar compliance frameworks.
* Experience in SaaS/product/platform environments.
* Experience with data analysis, operational metrics, or pattern‑identification.
* Exposure to incident management tools, quality systems, or technical support environments.



  





 Even if you don't meet all of the requirements for this role, we encourage you to apply! We are looking for talented and passionate individuals who are eager to learn and grow.
 



  





**Our interview process** 




 ✌ Benifex understands the need to have a fast and efficient process, the below will all be completed in the shortest time possible.
 



 📞 Initial call with the Talent team - 30 min
 



 🧪 Online tests in numeracy and logical reasoning
 



 ⏳ Interview with Operations Quality Lead - 60 min
 



 🤩 Final interview with a senior manager - 30-45 min
 



  





***Please let us know if you need any reasonable adjustments for interviews by replying to the email you will receive after you apply.***




  





**We are an equal opportunity employer and value diversity at our company. We do not discriminate on the basis of race, religion, colour, national origin, gender, sexual orientation, age, marital status, veteran status, or disability status.** 




  





**We are committed to creating a diverse and inclusive workplace where everyone feels welcome and respected. We believe that diversity and inclusion are essential to our success, and we are proud to be an equal opportunity employer.**
